common.copyFailed: Copy failed. Check your browser permissions.
common.name: Name

errors.network: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission for this action.
errors.server: The server returned an error. Try again later.

errorBoundary.title: Something broke
errorBoundary.body: The UI hit an unexpected error. Reload the page or return to the overview.

listFilter.noResults: No entries match your search or filters.

nav.subtitle: Dashboard
nav.footer: Localhost tooling only. No authentication — use on trusted machines.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Choose the UI language and the data directory used for CAs, gates, SQLite, and related files.
settings.languageDesc: Dashboard UI language. Stored in the browser’s localStorage.
settings.themeDesc: Light or dark interface. Stored in the browser’s localStorage.

home.title: Overview
home.description: See your gates and local CAs at a glance, and jump to hosts, passwords, and other tools.
home.quickContentBody: Serve any folder over HTTP with n0va. Like Gate, it runs in the dashboard process and stops when the dashboard exits.
home.quickGateBody: Configure TCP/TLS reverse proxy routes and start or stop listeners.
home.quickHostsBody: View and update the system hosts file with an elevation prompt on save.
home.quickCaBody: Create multiple local CAs and issue server certificates (PEM / PFX) per CA.
home.quickPwdBody: Generate strong passwords using the built-in random generator.
home.aboutIntro: n0va is a small async HTTP stack with optional TCP/TLS forwarding (Gate), certificate helpers, and password utilities — exposed here through a single Dashboard UI.
home.aboutGate: Gate — SNI-based TLS ingress (dashboard editor); upstream pools and per-route chunk hooks.
home.aboutCa: Local CA — Multiple OpenSSL-backed CAs, each with its own issued server certificates (PEM and PFX).
home.aboutPwd: Password — Uses Python secrets for generation.
home.apiDesc: SQLite path used by the Dashboard backend.

contentServers.title: Local static hosting
contentServers.description: Expose a folder with n0va’s built-in HTTP stack. Like Gate, each entry runs as an asyncio task in the dashboard process and stops when the dashboard stops.
contentServers.formHint: Use an absolute directory path. You cannot use the same host:port as this dashboard.
contentServers.rootHelp: Files under this folder are served from URL /. (e.g. index.html).
contentServers.autoStart: Start automatically when the dashboard starts
contentServers.errPort: Port must be an integer from 1 to 65535.
contentServers.errRoot: Please enter the document root path.
contentServers.confirmDelete: Delete static server #{{id}}? This cannot be undone.
contentServers.emptyBody: Add a folder to serve over local HTTP — handy for dev sites or build output.
contentServers.tip: Tip: binding to 0.0.0.0 may be reachable on your LAN. Open links use 127.0.0.1 when the listen address is all-interfaces. Edit settings only while stopped. Same in-process model as Gate.

gates.title: Gate
gates.description: TCP ingress proxies with TLS (SNI) termination: listen on an address and forward to upstream pools per hostname. Configure each gate on its own page — stop a gate before editing.
gates.confirmDelete: Delete gate #{{id}}? This cannot be undone.
gates.emptyBody: Create a gate to listen on a port and forward traffic to one or more upstreams. Use the form-based editor — no raw JSON required.
gates.tip: Tip: TLS termination requires PEM file paths on this machine. For upstreams that speak TLS, set "Upstream TLS SNI" on the route.

gateForm.headerDesc: TLS (SNI) termination only; each hostname maps to an upstream pool. Stop the gate before changing configuration.
gateForm.leaveUnsavedBody: You have unsaved changes. Leave this page?
gateForm.runningBanner: This gate is <0>running</0>. Stop it to edit the configuration below.
gateForm.confirmDelete: Delete gate #{{id}}? This cannot be undone.

sections.identityDesc: Display name used in the dashboard only.
sections.listenDesc: Where clients connect first. The dashboard configures TLS (SNI) entrances only.
sections.entranceDescTlsOnlyBefore: TLS (SNI) terminates TLS per hostname. PEM paths can be filled from certificates issued in
sections.noCertsHintBefore: No certificates in the dashboard yet. Issue one under
sections.selectIssuedAria: Apply issued certificate to SNI row {{n}}
sections.optionChoose: — Choose to fill cert & key paths —
sections.routesDesc: Connections are matched by route key, then forwarded only to that row’s upstream pool.
sections.routeKeySelectHint: Pick one of the SNI hostnames above (must match the client’s SNI).
sections.upstreamTlsPlaceholder: Leave empty for plain TCP to upstream

httpDispatch.title: HTTP-based upstream selection
httpDispatch.summary: When enabled, the first HTTP/1.x request after TLS termination (method and path) is used to pick an upstream using the rules below in order. Not suitable for non-HTTP traffic.
httpDispatch.enable: Switch upstreams by HTTP request (path and method rules)
httpDispatch.help: Rules are evaluated from top to bottom; the first match wins. If none match, the default upstream is used.

flow.title: Connection flow (overview)
flow.subtitle: How a client connection is accepted and forwarded. Edit values in each section below.
flow.listenDetail: Accepts TCP on this address and port.
flow.plainEntranceDetail: Default logical key is “{{key}}” (align route keys below).
flow.tlsEntranceCaption: Client SNI is matched against route keys below.
flow.plainEntranceCaption: Bytes are forwarded as-is. A single “*” route is often enough.
flow.routesIntro: Only connections matching a route key are sent to the listed upstreams.
flow.routeCardExplain: Only connections matching this key are load-balanced across the servers below.
flow.sniBlockExplain: For TLS, hostnames here are matched to the client’s SNI. Use the same names as your route keys.

ca.title: Local CA
ca.description: Manage multiple local certificate authorities under one data directory. Each CA has its own folder and issued certificates.
ca.dataDirDesc: Files live under a hidden `.n0va` folder below the data directory you set (e.g. {{pattern}}). The SQLite database is also under `.n0va`.
ca.createDesc: Adds a new root CA (PEM key + cert) in its own subdirectory. The CA private key is stored unencrypted for local development only.
ca.empty: No certificate authorities yet. Create one above.

caDetail.keysNote: By default, keys are unencrypted PEM files for local development only. Protect the data directory like any secret material.
caDetail.legacyPassphrase: A passphrase is stored for this CA (legacy). Do not commit secrets to version control.
caDetail.dangerDesc: Removes the CA folder and database rows for issued certs.
caDetail.confirmDelete: Delete this CA and all issued certificate records? Files under its folder will be removed.
caDetail.issueDesc: Signed by this CA. Files are written under ca/{{id}}/issued/ with a unique suffix.
caDetail.confirmDeleteIssued: Remove the record and PEM/PFX files for CN "{{cn}}" (serial {{serial}})? This cannot be undone.

hosts.title: Hosts
hosts.description: View and edit the system hosts file. Reading usually works as a normal user; saving triggers a one-time administrator elevation (UAC on Windows) — the Dashboard process itself does not need to run as admin.
hosts.elevNote: Saving will request elevation for this OS. Approve the prompt only if you trust this change.
hosts.msgSaved: Saved. On Windows, approve the UAC prompt for the elevated copy to succeed.
hosts.confirmSaveBody: This overwrites your system hosts file. Administrator approval may be required.
hosts.readOnlyAlert: This file cannot be edited in this session. Reload after fixing permissions, or edit outside the dashboard.
hosts.cannotSaveReadOnly: Cannot save while the file is read-only here.

password.title: Password
password.description: Generate passwords with cryptographically strong randomness (Python secrets). Choose a strength preset, custom character classes and length, or browser-style formats.
password.strengthSectionDesc: Length and character sets are fixed per preset. One click to generate.
password.strengthLowDesc: Low: length 12, upper, lower, digits (no symbols)
password.customSectionDesc: Choose upper, lower, digits, symbols and length. At least one character from each enabled class is included.
password.needOneClass: Enable at least one character class.
password.browserSectionDesc: Safari uses hyphen-separated groups; Firefox-style uses 15 characters with letters, digits, and symbols.
password.ariaMasked: Password is hidden. Use the show button to reveal it.
password.hint: A new generation replaces the value below. Store secrets in a vault, not in chat logs.

gate.listenPort: Listen port must be between 1 and 65535.
gate.backlog: Backlog must be a positive number.
gate.readLimit: Read limit must be at least 1024 bytes.
gate.tlsNoHost: TLS (SNI): add at least one hostname with certificate paths.
gate.tlsMissingPaths: TLS (SNI): {{host}} needs both cert file and key file paths.
gate.routeKey: Choose a route key (SNI hostname) for each route.
gate.routeUpstream: Route "{{key}}" needs at least one upstream.
gate.routeKeyNotSni: Route key "{{key}}" must match one of the entrance SNI hostnames.
gate.httpDefaultUpstream: Route "{{key}}": invalid HTTP dispatch default upstream.
gate.httpRuleUpstream: Route "{{key}}": HTTP rule {{n}} upstream index is out of range.
gate.httpRulePath: Route "{{key}}": HTTP rule {{n}} requires a path.
